page: Microsoft Azure D32s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dsv7 server family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. The instance provides 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It features 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and INT8 operations.

This server configuration does not include local storage, requiring external Premium Storage attachments for persistent disk operations. It does not feature any integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The virtualization layer is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

In benchmark evaluations, the D32s_v7 demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core tests, and top 10% for single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. Multi-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng multi-core and passmark database operations scoring in the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks also fall within the average range. For machine learning workloads, the hardware delivers strong prompt processing speeds for small and medium models, though text generation for large models is weak, placing in the bottom 25%.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D32s_v7 offers a balanced resource density suitable for general-purpose enterprise workloads. The combination of dedicated Intel Xeon cores and a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it appropriate for web serving, redis operations, and moderate serialization or decompression tasks. It is less suited for large-scale LLM text generation or workloads requiring high-speed local NVMe storage.
description: Microsoft Azure D32s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dsv7 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor on the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio. The instance lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, relying on external Premium Storage. Benchmark data reveals strong single-core CPU performance, with top-tier results in single-threaded compression and decompression. Multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. While the server handles prompt processing for small and medium language models efficiently, text generation for large models is weak. This configuration is suited for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, redis operations, and standard database tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32s_v7 is an x86_64 general-purpose server with 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong single-core performance but lacking local storage.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32s_v7 is a general-purpose VM featuring 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, strong single-core performance, and a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio.
bullet_points: Powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores.
Equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory and a 480 MB L3 cache.
Delivers strong single-core performance and top-tier single-threaded compression speeds.
Lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, requiring external storage solutions.
Best suited for static web serving, redis operations, and general-purpose database workloads.
